tidyMacro 1.0.0
  • Reference
    • Overview
    • tidyMacro

    • Replication: Bloom (2009)
    • Replication: Galí (1999)
    • Replication: Beaudry and Portier (2014)
    • Replication: Kaenzig (2021)

Kaenzig (2021) Data

Source: R/data.R
Kaenzig2021.Rd

Monthly data on global oil markets and US macroeconomic aggregates used to replicate the OPEC production cut shock identification of Kaenzig (2021). Includes a high-frequency external instrument based on oil futures price changes around OPEC announcements.

Usage

Kaenzig2021

Format

A data frame with rows and 8 columns:

Date

Month start date (Date)

Oil_Price

Log real oil price, in percent

World_Oil_Prod

Log world oil production, in percent

World_Oil_Inven

Log world oil inventories, in percent

World_IP

Log world industrial production, in percent

US_IP

Log US industrial production, in percent

US_CPI

Log US Consumer Price Index, in percent

iv_kanzig_final

High-frequency external instrument: oil futures price change in a narrow window around OPEC announcements. Contains NA outside announcement dates.

Details

The structural shock is identified using the external instrument iv_kanzig_final via the proxy-SVAR / IV approach. The instrument isolates exogenous variation in oil supply caused by OPEC production decisions.

References

Kaenzig, D. A. (2021). The macroeconomic effects of oil supply news: Evidence from OPEC announcements. American Economic Review, 111(4), 1092–1125.

Examples

data("Kaenzig2021")
head(Kaenzig2021)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 8
#>   Date       Oil_Price World_Oil_Prod World_Oil_Inven World_IP US_IP US_CPI
#>   <date>         <dbl>          <dbl>           <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>
#> 1 1974-01-01      307.          1092.            628.     379.  385.   385.
#> 2 1974-02-01      306.          1093.            632.     378.  385.   386.
#> 3 1974-03-01      305.          1094.            631.     378.  385.   387.
#> 4 1974-04-01      305.          1095.            634.     378.  385.   387.
#> 5 1974-05-01      304.          1096.            638.     379.  385.   388.
#> 6 1974-06-01      303.          1095.            640.     378.  385.   389.
#> # ℹ 1 more variable: iv_kanzig_final <dbl>
y <- Kaenzig2021 |>
  dplyr::select(Oil_Price, World_Oil_Prod, World_Oil_Inven,
                World_IP, US_IP, US_CPI) |>
  as.matrix()
